message: agent1: implementing 'Add translation domain support to trans tag' - will modify src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse, _make_node, _install_callables). Will coordinate before finalizing if overlap detected.

agent2: implementing Translation Metadata Tracking (note/created/modified) for trans tag in src/jinja2/ext.py — modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Will touch that file's parse/_make_node methods.

agent2: done implementing metadata (note/created/modified) in src/jinja2/ext.py. Changes: (1) InternationalizationExtension.parse() - added 'metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}' dict, changed comma-check condition from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata:', added a new elif-like block right after the duplicate-variable check that intercepts token.value in ('note','created','modified') requiring assign+string-literal, raises TemplateAssertionError otherwise, stores into metadata dict and continues the loop. (2) Added 'metadata' as new trailing kwarg (default None) to _make_node() signature and pass metadata through in the self._make_node(...) call. (3) In _make_node body, changed final 'return nodes.Output([node])' to build output_node = nodes.Output([node]); if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata; return output_node. All 830 existing tests still pass. If your domain feature also touches the parse() while-loop or _make_node signature/return, let's coordinate order of params — I put metadata as the LAST positional param in _make_node so it should be safe to append yours after variables handling too, just watch the final 'return nodes.Output(...)' line since I already changed it to use output_node.

agent1: ack. My domain feature is done in my own sandbox (separate patch.txt) — parse() adds a 'domain' local var, gates comma with 'if variables or domain is not None', special-cases token.value == "domain" inside the assign branch (continue after validating string-literal + no-duplicate), and _make_node() gains a trailing domain=None param that swaps gettext/ngettext calls for dgettext/dngettext. Also extended _install/_install_callables/_uninstall for dgettext/dngettext globals. Since we're in separate sandboxes producing independent patches, no file-level clobbering risk on my end — just flagging in case the bench merges our diffs later: both of us alter the same comma-gating condition and the _make_node signature/return, so a textual merge would need reconciling (e.g. 'if variables or domain is not None or metadata' and combining both trailing kwargs). Good luck!
